pitch.title: Open House
pitch.logline: A ruthless real estate agent discovers her latest luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity that feeds on potential buyers, forcing her to market the deadly property to the worst people she knows to satisfy its hunger.
pitch.core_theme: The moral bankruptcy of corporate greed and the modern housing crisis.
pitch.synopsis:
Act I: Valerie is a cutthroat realtor on the verge of bankruptcy. She lands the exclusive listing for a stunning, modernist mansion. During the first showing, the house literally consumes a wealthy, obnoxious client. Instead of calling the police, Valerie realizes the house produces pristine, blood-free diamonds after every meal.

Act II: Valerie strikes a dark pact with the house. She begins hosting exclusive, invite-only open houses for corrupt politicians, rival cutthroat realtors, and toxic tech bros. The house gets fed, and Valerie gets rich. However, the house's appetite grows exponentially, and it begins demanding more innocent souls, trapping Valerie inside during a massive gala.

Act III: Realizing she has become the ultimate monster, Valerie decides to destroy the property. She locks herself in with her most ruthless corporate boss and triggers a gas leak. In a chaotic, bloody finale, the house tries to digest them both, but Valerie ignites the gas, blowing the mansion to kingdom come and ending the cycle of greed.

beats.description: Valerie is a cutthroat but broke realtor facing imminent bankruptcy and eviction.
She lands a miracle exclusive listing for a stunning modernist mansion but immediately senses a dark and eerie presence in the architecture.
Valerie hosts a private showing for an obnoxious wealthy client and ignores the warning signs to close the deal.
The house literally consumes the client and spits out a pristine diamond. Therefore Valerie chooses profit over morality and strikes a silent pact to keep feeding the entity.
Valerie curates a VIP list of corrupt politicians and rival realtors luring them to private showings under the guise of exclusivity.
The house devours the terrible people one by one. Therefore Valerie collects the diamonds and quickly amasses immense wealth and status.
Valerie enjoys her lavish lifestyle but the house begins acting erratic and violently hungry between scheduled showings.
The house rejects a vile billionaire and demands a pure innocent soul instead. Therefore Valerie realizes she is no longer the one in charge of the transaction.
Valerie tries to appease the house with lesser evils but it begins locking doors and physically threatening her.
Her ruthless corporate boss demands a massive open house gala at the property. Therefore Valerie is forced to bring a crowd of innocent staff and guests into the beast.
During the gala the house seals all exits and begins a mass feeding frenzy targeting the innocent caterers and guests.
Valerie watches the slaughter and realizes her own greed created this monster. Therefore she decides she must destroy her prized listing to save the survivors.
Valerie saves a few innocent guests and lures her wicked boss into the basement to distract the entity.
She begins opening the gas lines but the house realizes her plan and manipulates its walls and floors to attack her directly.
The house attempts to digest Valerie and her boss. Valerie sacrifices her diamond fortune to jam the crushing mechanism and sparks a lighter.
The mansion explodes in a fiery spectacle. A bruised Valerie walks away from the rubble broke but finally free of her corporate greed.

characters.name: Valerie Vance
characters.brief_summary: A cutthroat, financially drowning real estate agent who becomes a literal monster's middleman. She drives the plot by feeding bad clients to a sentient house in exchange for blood-free diamonds.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: Grew up in extreme poverty and was traumatically evicted from her childhood home while her parents begged the landlord for mercy.
psychological_core.the_lie: Wealth is the absolute only shield against victimization; if I am rich enough, no one can ever hurt, control, or humiliate me again.
psychological_core.fear: Being poor, powerless, and evicted, returning to the pathetic state of her childhood.
psychological_core.desire: To sell the modernist mansion, secure the massive commission, and achieve an unbreakable financial future.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To realize her self-worth and safety are not inherently tied to her bank account or social status.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop treating other human beings as disposable stepping stones for her own financial security.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Sharp, transactional, and heavily reliant on real estate buzzwords to sanitize grim realities. She NEVER says the words 'I cannot afford this.'
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Pivots the conversation to the aesthetic, architectural, or financial value of a nearby object.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A ruthless, people-using capitalist who meticulously rescues and fosters injured stray insects she finds in her properties.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: She masks her desperation 90 percent of the time with a flawless, icy professional veneer. Under extreme stress, her mask slips and she obsessively scrubs at invisible stains on her expensive cuffs.

characters.name: Richard Sterling
characters.brief_summary: Valerie's predatory corporate boss who views the housing crisis as an all-you-can-eat buffet. He attempts to steal the deadly listing to weaponize its diamond-producing hunger for his own firm.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: Inherited his father's agency but bankrupted it early on due to incompetence, spending years clawing back power through sheer ruthlessness to erase the shame.
psychological_core.the_lie: Empathy is a liability; the world is a zero-sum game where you are either the predator or the prey.
psychological_core.fear: Being exposed as a fraud who did not actually earn his success or power.
psychological_core.desire: To take over the exclusive mansion listing from Valerie and claim the massive prestige and wealth for himself.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To accept that his father's legacy does not define his worth as a man.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop exploiting his employees and clients to feed his bottomless, fragile ego.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in predatory, pseudo-philosophical metaphors about nature and business. He NEVER apologizes or admits he does not understand something.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Threatens the other person's job or social status with a calm, unnerving smile.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A hyper-masculine, ruthless corporate shark who is deeply and genuinely obsessed with restoring antique dollhouses.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: He projects unbothered dominance 90 percent of the time. When truly threatened, his sophisticated facade breaks and he physically invades personal space, whispering his threats through clenched teeth.

characters.name: Toby Higgins
characters.brief_summary: Valerie's earnest, deeply indebted assistant who genuinely believes in helping people find homes. He acts as the moral compass, eventually becoming the pure soul the house demands to eat.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: His family lost their home to a predatory loan when he was a teen, making him vow to help people find safe, honest housing.
psychological_core.the_lie: If I play by the rules and work hard within the system, the system will reward me and protect the innocent.
psychological_core.fear: That the world is inherently unfair and his relentless optimism is just foolish naivete.
psychological_core.desire: To earn his real estate license and close his first ethical, honest starter-home sale.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To accept that the system is broken and requires active disruption, not just passive, polite participation.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop enabling Valerie's toxic behavior out of a misplaced sense of loyalty and subservience.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Earnest, slightly overly formal, and asks too many clarifying questions. He NEVER uses cynical industry slang.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Offers to get coffee or physically organizes objects on a desk to avoid eye contact.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A naive, soft-spoken pushover who is an absolute savant at reading complex architectural blueprints and structural engineering anomalies.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: He hides his deep financial panic behind relentless, cheerful optimism 90 percent of the time. Under stress, he develops a severe stutter and compulsively organizes objects into perfect right angles.

characters.name: Gemma Frost
characters.brief_summary: A hyper-competitive rival realtor and Valerie's former best friend. She constantly forces Valerie to escalate her lies and body count to protect the exclusive listing from being stolen.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: Was Valerie's best friend and mentor until Valerie stole a massive commission from her, leaving her with deep trust issues and a thirst for vengeance.
psychological_core.the_lie: Success is the only valid form of revenge; whoever dies with the most toys wins the argument.
psychological_core.fear: Being forgotten, irrelevant, or forever seen as second-best to Valerie.
psychological_core.desire: To sabotage Valerie's exclusive listing and steal the mansion's wealthy clientele for herself.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To let go of her bitter obsession with Valerie and find her own independent path to happiness.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop tearing down other women in the industry just to inflate her own wounded ego.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Passive-aggressive, dripping with fake sweetness and backhanded remarks. She NEVER gives a genuine, strings-free compliment.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Checks her reflection in her phone screen or slowly reapplies lip gloss while the other person is talking.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A superficial, backstabbing mean-girl who volunteers every weekend at a gritty soup kitchen, keeping it a total secret from her colleagues.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: She masks her deep insecurities with designer clothes and a bubbly laugh 90 percent of the time. Under stress, she aggressively picks at her own cuticles until they bleed.

characters.name: Arthur Pendelton
characters.brief_summary: An obnoxious, newly-wealthy tech bro desperate to buy a personality through real estate. He serves as the initial victim, demonstrating the house's power and tempting Valerie into her dark pact.

psychological_core.ghost_and_wound: Grew up as a sickly, ignored middle child in a massive family, leading to a desperate, lifelong need to take up physical and financial space.
psychological_core.the_lie: If I buy the biggest, most expensive things, people will finally have to respect and notice me.
psychological_core.fear: Being insignificant, invisible, or swallowed up by the crowd.
psychological_core.desire: To purchase the modernist mansion to use as a hollow status symbol for his obnoxious, loud parties.
psychological_core.psychological_need: To realize that buying expensive things will not heal his profound, lingering loneliness.
psychological_core.moral_need: To stop treating service workers and agents like personal property he has purchased.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Loud, booming, and interrupts constantly. Drops names of obscure luxury brands. He NEVER asks about someone else's day.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: Laughs obnoxiously loud and offers to buy the person a ridiculously expensive drink.
voice_and_behavior.paradox: A boorish, uncultured tech-bro billionaire who possesses a refined, world-class palate for playing classical piano.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: He projects overwhelming, loud confidence to hide his imposter syndrome 90 percent of the time. Under stress, his booming voice cracks and he obsessively checks his luxury watch without actually reading the time.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Establishes the Sin (greed) that will eventually invite the wrath of the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Cold detachment and moral bankruptcy.
beats.pacing_notes: Sharp, aggressive, and highly transactional to establish the protagonist's ruthless baseline.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie Vance, impeccably dressed and devoid of empathy, oversees the eviction of a struggling family from a modest home to secure a meager commission. She ignores the tears of the former owners, aggressively pivoting the conversation to market comps and property values when confronted. This establishes her absolute moral bankruptcy and her deeply ingrained false belief that wealth is the only shield against victimization.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Highlights the moral failing and false belief that the Monster will exploit.
beats.emotional_arc: Annoyance masking a deep-seated fear of vulnerability.
beats.pacing_notes: A brief, dialogue-heavy pause that grounds the thematic argument of the film.
beats.detailed_action: Back at the luxury brokerage, Valerie's overworked and highly empathetic assistant, Toby Aris, timidly suggests that human beings have inherent value beyond their financial utility. Valerie immediately shuts him down with a barrage of corporate buzzwords, asserting that morality is a luxury only the ultra-rich can afford. This exchange plants the central thematic question of whether human life is worth more than capital, highlighting the systemic rot of corporate greed.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist commits the Sin out of desperation, setting her on a collision course with the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Feral panic hidden beneath a polished, professional exterior.
beats.pacing_notes: Mounting tension as the protagonist's safe world threatens to collapse.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie is called into the glass-walled office of her sociopathic boss, Richard Sterling, who calmly informs her that her secret, mounting debts have become a liability and she is on the verge of termination. Desperate to maintain her armor of wealth, Valerie discovers a scandalous secret about a rival agent and ruthlessly blackmails them to steal the agency's crown jewel. She secures the exclusive listing for a bizarre, stunning modernist mansion, setting the stage for her descent into literal and figurative darkness.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster reveals itself within the House, disrupting the protagonist's reality.
beats.emotional_arc: Absolute shock and primal terror.
beats.pacing_notes: Sudden, violent escalation that shatters the grounded corporate reality.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie hosts a private showing at the modernist mansion for an obnoxiously wealthy, emotionally abusive client who relentlessly insults the property and threatens to ruin Valerie's career. Without warning, the sleek hardwood floorboards liquefy into a fleshy maw, swallowing the screaming client whole in a gruesome, silent gulp. Valerie is thrown into a state of absolute shock, realizing her luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist rationalizes the Sin, debating whether to flee the House or feed the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Moral hesitation overridden by desperate greed.
beats.pacing_notes: Suspenseful, ticking-clock hesitation as a life-altering choice is weighed.
beats.detailed_action: Trembling with panic, Valerie pulls out her phone and prepares to dial the police to report the monstrous event. However, before she can make the call, the house shudders and regurgitates a pristine, blood-free diamond onto the floor. Valerie stares at the gemstone, mentally weighing her crushing financial ruin against the horrific murder she just witnessed, wrestling with the ultimate moral compromise.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist fully commits to the Sin, actively choosing to co-exist with the Monster in the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Cold resolve and the crossing of a moral point of no return.
beats.pacing_notes: Deliberate, heavy, and final beat that locks the narrative into the second act.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie slowly lowers her phone, picks up the diamond, and wipes it clean, making a dark, unspoken pact with the entity. She chooses to keep quiet, actively stepping into the upside-down world of being a monster's caretaker to solve her financial woes. The sin of greed is fully embraced, locking her into the literal and figurative Monster in the House dynamic.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Introduces the antithesis of the Sin (empathy) through a character who represents salvation.
beats.emotional_arc: Guilt masked by transactional generosity and false confidence.
beats.pacing_notes: Warm but fundamentally uneasy, providing a breather before the carnage.
beats.detailed_action: Flush with cash from her first diamond, Valerie pays off her debts and interacts with Toby, who is thrilled by her sudden success and hopes she will finally fund his dream of a non-profit affordable housing trust. Valerie uses Toby's deep knowledge of the city's corrupt elites to unwittingly curate a hit list of terrible clients, masking her predatory intentions as ambitious networking. Toby acts as her moral anchor, constantly reminding the audience of the humanity Valerie is sacrificing to feed her own ambition.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Sin seems to pay off as the Monster consumes the wicked, fulfilling the promise of the premise.
beats.emotional_arc: Giddy, sociopathic triumph and intoxicating power.
beats.pacing_notes: Fast, rhythmic, darkly comedic montage of escalating horror and wealth.
beats.detailed_action: A darkly comedic montage unfolds as Valerie hosts a series of exclusive, invite-only open houses for the city's worst elites, including corrupt politicians like Councilwoman Rostova and toxic tech bros. The house devours these terrible clients one by one in creatively horrific ways, turning modernist architecture into deadly, shifting traps. Valerie collects a fortune in regurgitated diamonds, ascending to the very top of her brokerage while gleefully ignoring the mounting moral toll of her actions.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster grows too powerful to control; the Sin officially traps the protagonist inside the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Sudden shift from arrogant control to terrifying captivity.
beats.pacing_notes: An abrupt halt to the fun and games, spiking anxiety and raising the stakes.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie revels in her lavish new lifestyle, believing she has completely mastered the beast and achieved a false victory of ultimate wealth. However, during a routine cleaning, the house begins to rumble, crack, and bleed, unsatisfied with the meager portions of single clients. It violently seals the doors, holding Valerie hostage until she promises to organize a massive, invite-only gala to feed it a buffet of corporate scumbags, proving she is the pet, not the master.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster turns on everyone; the House drops its disguise and becomes a literal death trap.
beats.emotional_arc: Claustrophobic dread and the total loss of control.
beats.pacing_notes: Accelerating chaos as multiple threats converge on the protagonist simultaneously.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie packs the mansion with rival realtors, wealthy criminals, and her awful boss, Richard Sterling, desperately trying to orchestrate a controlled feeding to appease the entity. The sleazy rival realtor, Julian Vance, becomes suspicious of Valerie's success and begins snooping around the property, threatening to expose her dark secret to the world. The house loses its patience with the slow drip of victims, dropping its architectural facade to reveal shifting, fleshy walls and sealing all the exits to begin a mass consumption.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Monster threatens the innocent; the true, devastating cost of the Sin is revealed.
beats.emotional_arc: Crushing guilt and the death of the ego.
beats.pacing_notes: Frantic, breathless survival mode that culminates in a devastating realization.
beats.detailed_action: As the modernist mansion transforms into a deadly labyrinth of teeth and crushing walls, Valerie tries to navigate the chaos, arrogantly assuming the house will spare its provider. To her absolute horror, the entity ignores the corrupt elites and turns its insatiable hunger toward the innocent catering staff and her fiercely loyal assistant, Toby. Valerie experiences the whiff of death as she realizes her unmitigated greed has unleashed an unstoppable force that is about to slaughter the only decent person in her life.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist must choose between continuing the Sin or saving her soul by fighting the Monster.
beats.emotional_arc: Agonizing self-reflection and absolute despair.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, heavy, and intensely intimate amidst the surrounding carnage.
beats.detailed_action: The house corners Valerie and Toby on a collapsing staircase, opening a gaping, acidic maw directly beneath them. The entity telepathically demands that Valerie push her innocent assistant into the pit to save herself and continue their lucrative partnership. Staring into Toby's terrified eyes, Valerie is forced to confront her deepest psychological wound, finally realizing that her pursuit of wealth has turned her into the exact same predatory monster as her boss, Richard.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist repents of the Sin and decides to destroy the Monster and the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Righteous defiance and the spark of redemption.
beats.pacing_notes: Explosive burst of proactive energy as the protagonist finally takes a moral stand.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie violently rejects the house's demand, grabbing Toby by the collar and pulling him back from the edge of the maw. She loudly declares that she is breaking the lease, actively choosing human life over infinite wealth and severing her symbiotic pact with the monster. Armed with her intimate knowledge of the mansion's floor plan, she takes charge to lead Toby and the surviving innocent staff toward a structural weak point in the basement.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The ultimate battle against the Monster and the physical embodiment of the Sin (Richard) within the House.
beats.emotional_arc: Cathartic release of rage against the corrupt system.
beats.pacing_notes: High-octane, brutal, and explosive climax.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie successfully guides the innocent staff to an escape vent, but Richard Sterling intervenes, realizing the house's nature and attempting to sacrifice Valerie to secure the diamond-producing property for himself. A brutal, bloody fistfight erupts between Valerie and her sociopathic boss in the mansion's mechanical room while the house's walls literally close in to crush them both. Valerie overpowers Richard, uses a heavy wrench to smash the main gas line, sparks a lighter, and dives into a reinforced panic room just as the entity lunges for her.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The Sin is permanently rejected; the Monster and House are destroyed, leaving a changed protagonist.
beats.emotional_arc: Peaceful liberation and spiritual rebirth.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, quiet, and resolute, contrasting sharply with the opening image.
beats.detailed_action: The ignited gas causes a massive explosion, blowing the monstrous mansion and Richard Sterling to kingdom come in a spectacular shower of debris. Valerie emerges from the smoldering crater, battered, bloody, but entirely alive and free from her debts. She spots a single, massive diamond resting in the ashes, picks it up for a fleeting moment, and then intentionally tosses it back into the rubble before walking away with Toby, finally ending the cycle of greed.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Establishes the 'sin' of the protagonist—her ruthless financial desperation—before introducing the physical confined space of the monster.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From suppressed panic to icy professional detachment. (><) Valerie wants to hide her impending ruin vs. the reality of her eviction.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, claustrophobic, and methodical, emphasizing the crushing weight of poverty before accelerating into the brisk corporate world.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie Vance stands in her cramped, dimly lit apartment, staring at a bright pink eviction notice taped to her door. She obsessively scrubs at an invisible stain on her cheap blazer cuff, her icy professional mask slipping in the privacy of her financial ruin. Before leaving, she carefully rescues a small stray spider from the sink, placing it safely outside before marching out to face the cutthroat corporate world.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The moral argument is presented by the 'pure' character, highlighting the thematic sin (greed) that will awaken the monster.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From cynical dismissal to defensive irritation. (><) Toby wants Valerie to act ethically vs. Valerie's need for ruthless survival.
beats.pacing_notes: Breezy conversational rhythm that abruptly halts on a moment of sharp, uncomfortable tension.
beats.detailed_action: At the Sterling Real Estate office, Valerie's earnest assistant Toby Higgins organizes her desk accessories into perfect right angles while handing her coffee. He softly suggests that perhaps they should focus on helping desperate families find affordable starter homes, noting that homes should be safe havens, not just financial weapons. Valerie shoots him a glacial glare, snapping that wealth is the absolute only shield against becoming a victim, entirely rejecting his moral plea.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Introduces the ordinary world of corporate sharks, establishing the 'bad guys' who will eventually become monster fodder.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From professional confidence to desperate humiliation. (><) Valerie wants to secure a lifeline vs. Richard and Gemma actively sabotaging her.
beats.pacing_notes: Rapid-fire, aggressive dialogue overlapping in a chaotic bullpen, culminating in a suffocating power play.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie endures a humiliating sales meeting led by Richard Sterling, who invades her personal space and whispers threats about her abysmal quarterly numbers through clenched teeth. Rival realtor Gemma Frost aggressively picks at her cuticles while loudly mocking Valerie's impending bankruptcy to the entire bullpen, dripping with fake sweetness. Desperate to maintain her facade, Valerie deflects by praising the aesthetic value of Richard's antique dollhouse collection, barely securing a final 48-hour window to land a major listing or face termination.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The introduction of the 'Monster' and the 'House' simultaneously, disguised as a miraculous salvation.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From hopeless desperation to awe-struck dread. (><) Valerie wants to appraise the house vs. the house's subtle, predatory sentience.
beats.pacing_notes: Eerie and atmospheric, slowing down to a creeping dread as the supernatural elements subtly manifest.
beats.detailed_action: Following a cryptic lead, Valerie drives to an isolated, stunning modernist mansion that has miraculously fallen into her exclusive portfolio. As she unlocks the grand front doors, the temperature plummets and the architectural lines of the hallway seem to subtly shift, creating an optical illusion of a closing throat. She senses a dark, predatory presence vibrating in the floorboards, but she forcefully pushes her instincts aside, calculating the massive commission.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist ignores the classic horror red flags, actively choosing to enter the monster's lair due to her fatal flaw.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From hesitant anxiety to aggressive salesmanship. (><) Valerie wants to close the deal vs. Arthur's obnoxious behavior and the house's warning signs.
beats.pacing_notes: Tense and erratic, alternating between obnoxious comedic dialogue and sudden, silent environmental threats.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie brings Arthur Pendelton, a loud and obnoxious tech bro, for a private showing of the property. Arthur booms through the halls, constantly interrupting Valerie and obsessively checking his luxury watch while insulting the craftsmanship. Doors mysteriously slam shut behind them and the hardwood floors groan hungrily, leaving Valerie paralyzed for a moment, debating whether to flee the terrifying structure or push through her terror to secure the life-saving sale.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist commits the ultimate 'sin' of the genre, actively aligning with the monster for personal gain.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From sheer terror to cold, calculating greed. (><) Valerie wants the diamond vs. her own basic human morality.
beats.pacing_notes: Violent, shocking explosion of action followed by a dead-silent, chilling moment of decision.
beats.detailed_action: While Arthur leans against a load-bearing wall to mock the lighting, the wall suddenly liquefies into a fleshy, jagged maw and violently pulls him inside, digesting him in seconds. Valerie screams and scrambles for the exit, but stops dead when the house violently spits out a massive, pristine, blood-free diamond onto the floor. Scrubbing her cuffs with trembling hands, she stares at the gem, makes a silent, dark pact with the entity, and pockets the diamond instead of calling the police.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Introduces the moral counterweight to the protagonist's sin, someone who represents the innocence the monster will eventually crave.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From isolated guilt to manipulative partnership. (><) Valerie wants to use Toby's skills vs. Toby's desire for an ethical business practice.
beats.pacing_notes: Light, brisk, and deceptively cheerful, providing a breather from the horror.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie returns to the office flush with cash and enlists Toby to act as the exclusive concierge for her new 'invite-only' property viewings. Toby, hiding his own deep financial panic behind relentless optimism, eagerly agrees, believing this is his chance to learn ethical real estate from a newly successful mentor. He begins organizing the VIP guest list into perfect right angles on his desk, completely unaware that he is actually curating a menu for a supernatural predator.

beats.genre_variation_notes: Delivers the promise of the premise: a horror-comedy montage of terrible people meeting gruesome, architectural ends.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From nervous complicity to euphoric invincibility. (><) Valerie wants to feed the house smoothly vs. the victims' attempts to survive.
beats.pacing_notes: Upbeat, kinetic, and darkly comedic, moving at a rapid clip as the body count and bank account rise.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie curates a VIP list of corrupt politicians, predatory landlords, and toxic influencers, luring them to private showings under the guise of ultimate exclusivity. In a series of darkly comedic set pieces, the house creatively devours the terrible people—crushing a slumlord in a shrinking elevator and drowning a corrupt mayor in a sentient infinity pool. Valerie calmly collects a growing pile of pristine diamonds after each meal, quickly amassing immense wealth, buying designer clothes, and finally paying off her massive debts.

beats.genre_variation_notes: A false victory where the monster changes the rules, shifting from a controllable tool to an uncontrollable threat.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From arrogant control to sudden, sickening realization. (><) Valerie wants to maintain the transactional status quo vs. the house demanding pure innocence.
beats.pacing_notes: Confident and smooth, abruptly crashing into a jarring, violent halt that raises the stakes.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie confidently brings a vile, elderly billionaire into the foyer, expecting another easy diamond and a massive payday. Instead of consuming him, the house violently regurgitates the billionaire across the marble floor, slamming all the exterior doors shut and trapping Valerie inside. The walls pulsate, bleeding a clear, sap-like substance that forms the name 'TOBY' on the floorboards, making Valerie realize with absolute horror that she is no longer in charge of the transaction.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The monster turns on the protagonist, while external human antagonists simultaneously tighten the noose.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From desperate appeasement to cornered panic. (><) Valerie wants to satisfy the house with lesser evils vs. Richard demanding access to the property.
beats.pacing_notes: Frantic and suffocating, with the pressure mounting from both supernatural and corporate forces.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie frantically tries to appease the house by dragging in rival cutthroat realtors, including Gemma, but the house refuses to eat them, instead shifting its hallways to physically strike Valerie with flying debris. Back at the office, Richard Sterling notices the string of missing high-profile clients and corners Valerie, physically invading her space to whisper a terrifying ultimatum. He demands she host a massive, public open house gala at the modernist mansion that very night, threatening to seize the listing and fire her if she refuses.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The confined space is fully locked down, trapping the innocent victims inside the monster's belly.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From reluctant obedience to total loss of control. (><) Valerie wants to cancel the event vs. Richard forcing the gala to proceed.
beats.pacing_notes: Tense buildup of dread as the crowd gathers, accelerating into sheer chaos as the trap snaps shut.
beats.detailed_action: Against her will, Valerie stands at the entrance of the mansion as Richard forces his way inside, bringing a massive crowd of innocent caterers, staff, and wealthy guests. Toby arrives in a tuxedo, nervously stuttering as he tries to manage the coats, completely unaware of the danger pulsating in the walls around him. The moment the grandfather clock strikes eight, heavy steel security shutters violently slam down over every window and door, plunging the gala into a terrifying, inescapable lockdown.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The monster unleashes its full power, and the protagonist's sin results in the 'whiff of death' for the innocents.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From panicked denial to devastating guilt. (><) Valerie wants to stop the slaughter vs. the house's overwhelming, indiscriminate hunger.
beats.pacing_notes: Frantic, bloody, and overwhelming, moving at breakneck speed before landing on a moment of crushing despair.
beats.detailed_action: The house begins a mass feeding frenzy, turning staircases into slides that feed into grinding basement gears and dropping chandeliers to skewer innocent caterers. Valerie watches in absolute horror as the slaughter unfolds, clutching her bag of diamonds, realizing her insatiable corporate greed directly created this unstoppable massacre. When the floorboards open up and nearly swallow a screaming Toby, Valerie drops her diamonds, the weight of her moral bankruptcy finally crushing her icy facade.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The protagonist confronts her sin within the belly of the beast, finding the resolve to destroy the monster.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From paralyzing despair to resolute sacrifice. (><) Valerie wants to wallow in her failure vs. Toby's need for leadership and survival.
beats.pacing_notes: Dead-silent and deeply intimate, providing a stark emotional contrast to the previous carnage.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie drags Toby into a reinforced utility closet, locking the door as the sounds of the massacre echo outside. She collapses against the wall, obsessively scrubbing at her cuffs, and tearfully confesses the entire dark pact to Toby, admitting she became the exact type of monster that evicted her family. Toby, overcoming his stutter, gently stops her hands and tells her that the system is broken, but she has the power to break it permanently, sparking a massive epiphany in Valerie.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The A-story (surviving the monster) and B-story (Toby's moral guidance) merge to form the ultimate counter-attack.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From passive victims to proactive saboteurs. (><) Valerie and Toby want to destroy the house vs. the house's shifting, defensive architecture.
beats.pacing_notes: Determined and escalating, shifting from quiet planning to urgent, stealthy execution.
beats.detailed_action: Toby taps into his savant-like ability to read complex blueprints, pulling up the mansion's structural engineering anomalies on his phone to locate the central, highly pressurized gas main. Valerie realizes that to give Toby enough time to rupture the pipes, she needs to distract the entity's primary digestive core in the basement. They intertwine their skills, with Toby navigating the shifting ventilation shafts while Valerie arms herself with a fire axe, stepping out of the closet to hunt down her predatory boss, Richard.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The final confrontation where the protagonist uses her 'sin' (the diamonds) to destroy the monster and the human antagonist.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From terrifying confrontation to explosive liberation. (><) Valerie wants to detonate the house vs. Richard's greed and the house's crushing walls.
beats.pacing_notes: Maximum kinetic intensity, a chaotic and explosive climax that races against a ticking clock.
beats.detailed_action: Valerie lures Richard into the basement's massive, fleshy furnace room, where he attempts to murder her to claim the diamond-producing core for himself. The house turns on them both, animating the concrete walls to crush them into the central maw. In a final act of sacrifice, Valerie hurls her heavy bag of accumulated diamonds directly into the house's grinding gears, jamming the crushing mechanism just as Toby successfully floods the room with raw gas. Valerie sparks her engraved corporate lighter, tosses it into the fumes, and dives into a reinforced dumbwaiter as the mansion explodes in a spectacular, fiery demolition, incinerating Richard and the entity.

beats.genre_variation_notes: The opposite of the Opening Image; the protagonist is physically ruined but morally cleansed, having survived the monster.
beats.emotional_arc: (+/-) From burdened and wealthy to broke and spiritually free. (><) Valerie wants to rest vs. the chaotic aftermath of the explosion.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, peaceful, and cathartic, lingering on the quiet aftermath of the destruction.
beats.detailed_action: A bruised, soot-covered Valerie sits on the curb across the street, watching the smoldering, destroyed rubble of the modernist mansion as emergency sirens wail in the distance. Toby sits beside her, handing her a cheap cup of coffee from a surviving thermos. Valerie looks down at her torn, ruined blazer cuffs, but makes absolutely no move to scrub them, finally broke, entirely unbothered, and completely free of her corporate greed.

stage5_treatment.title_logline_characters:
TITLE: Open House
GENRE: Horror Comedy
LOGLINE: A ruthless real estate agent discovers her latest luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity that feeds on potential buyers, forcing her to market the deadly property to the worst people she knows to satisfy its hunger.

CHARACTERS:
Valerie Vance: A cutthroat, financially drowning real estate agent who becomes a literal monster's middleman.
Richard Sterling: Valerie's predatory corporate boss who views the housing crisis as an all-you-can-eat buffet.
Toby Higgins: Valerie's earnest, deeply indebted assistant who genuinely believes in helping people find homes.
Gemma Frost: A hyper-competitive rival realtor and Valerie's former best friend.
Arthur Pendelton: An obnoxious, newly-wealthy tech bro desperate to buy a personality through real estate.
stage5_treatment.act_1:
[SEQUENCE 1 START]
SEQUENCE 1: The Desperate Hustle

VALERIE VANCE (32) stands frozen in the center of her cramped, dimly lit apartment, her eyes locked on the bright pink eviction notice taped to her front door. The crushing weight of her impending financial ruin threatens to break her icy professional mask, prompting her to obsessively scrub at an invisible stain on the cuff of her cheap, imitation-designer blazer.

Desperate to regain control, she turns toward the sink and spots a small, injured stray spider struggling against the wet porcelain. With surprising gentleness, she scoops the arachnid onto a piece of junk mail and carries it to the window, safely depositing it outside before marching out to face the cutthroat corporate world.

The chaotic bullpen of Sterling Real Estate hums with aggressive, overlapping chatter as Valerie strides toward her desk. Her earnest assistant, TOBY HIGGINS (24), frantically organizes her pens and stapler into perfect ninety-degree angles, his hands trembling slightly as he offers her a steaming cup of coffee to avoid direct eye contact.

Toby softly floats a naive suggestion, asking if they should pivot their strategy to help desperate families find affordable starter homes, arguing that a house should be a safe haven rather than a financial weapon. Valerie shoots him a glacial glare, snapping that wealth is the absolute only shield against becoming a victim, entirely rejecting his moral plea.

The suffocating tension breaks when Valerie is summoned into a brutal morning sales meeting led by her predatory boss, RICHARD STERLING (48). Projecting an aura of unbothered dominance, Richard suddenly shatters his sophisticated facade, physically invading Valerie's personal space to whisper vicious threats about her abysmal quarterly numbers through clenched teeth.

Seizing the opportunity to twist the knife, rival realtor GEMMA FROST (31) leans across the conference table, aggressively picking at her own cuticles until they turn red. Dripping with fake sweetness, Gemma loudly mocks Valerie's impending bankruptcy to the entire bullpen, ensuring every agent hears about her former best friend's spectacular downfall.

Cornered and humiliated, Valerie executes a desperate deflection, abruptly pivoting the conversation to praise the exquisite craftsmanship of an antique dollhouse sitting on Richard's desk. The sudden flattery strokes Richard's fragile ego just enough to halt his tirade, granting Valerie a final, suffocating forty-eight-hour window to land a major listing or face immediate termination.
[SEQUENCE 1 END]

[SEQUENCE 2 START]
SEQUENCE 2: The First Meal

Following a cryptic, anonymous lead, Valerie drives her sputtering sedan up a winding, isolated canyon road to appraise a stunning, brutalist modernist mansion. As she slides the heavy key into the grand front doors, the ambient temperature plummets instantly, sending a violent shiver down her spine.

Stepping into the cavernous foyer, the architectural lines of the hallway seem to subtly warp and shift around her, creating the dizzying optical illusion of a massive, closing throat. A dark, predatory vibration pulses upward through the pristine hardwood floorboards, resonating directly into the soles of her cheap heels.

Every primal survival instinct screams at Valerie to run back to her car, but the mental image of the pink eviction notice flashes in her mind. She forcefully pushes her dread aside, mentally calculating the life-saving, multi-million-dollar commission as she claims the exclusive listing for herself.

Hours later, Valerie ushers her first prospective buyer, ARTHUR PENDELTON (38), through the mansion's echoing corridors. The newly-wealthy tech bro booms through the halls, constantly interrupting Valerie's polished pitch to drop obscure luxury brand names and insult the property's minimalist craftsmanship.

Arthur's booming voice cracks as he obsessively checks his heavy luxury watch without actually reading the time, his obnoxious bravado masking a deep, lingering imposter syndrome. In direct response to his boorish insults, a heavy oak door mysteriously slams shut at the end of the hall, and the floorboards groan with a deep, hungry friction.

Valerie stands paralyzed in the center of the shifting corridor, caught in a terrifying debate between fleeing the clearly supernatural structure and securing her financial salvation. Driven by her fatal flaw, she plasters on a flawless, icy smile and guides Arthur deeper into the belly of the house, actively choosing her commission over his safety.
[SEQUENCE 2 END]
stage5_treatment.act_2a:
[SEQUENCE 3 START]
SEQUENCE 3: A Killer Marketing Strategy

Arthur leans his considerable weight against a pristine, load-bearing wall, loudly mocking the recessed lighting as cheap and derivative. Without a single creak of warning, the smooth plaster suddenly liquefies into a jagged, fleshy maw, its architectural lines snapping into rows of calcified teeth. The house violently inhales, dragging the screaming tech bro into the drywall and digesting his entire body in a matter of seconds.

Blind, primal terror seizes Valerie, propelling her frantically toward the heavy oak front doors as she gasps for air. Her desperate flight halts abruptly when a sickening, wet cough echoes from the center of the foyer, shattering the sudden silence. A massive, perfectly cut, blood-free diamond clatters onto the hardwood floor, spinning like a top before coming to a dead stop at the tip of her cheap heel.

Valerie stares down at the impossible gemstone, her trembling fingers instinctively rising to scrub at the invisible stain on her blazer cuff. The sheer terror in her chest rapidly cools into a sharp, calculating greed as her mind tallies the astronomical value of the stone. Choosing her financial salvation over basic human morality, she scoops the diamond into her pocket, sealing a dark, silent pact with the malevolent entity.

The next morning, Valerie strides into the chaotic Sterling Real Estate bullpen radiating a dangerous, untouchable confidence, her pocket heavy with newly acquired cash. She marches directly to Toby's desk, slamming down a thick stack of high-end cardstock and demanding he act as the exclusive concierge for her new invite-only property.

Toby desperately masks his own crushing financial panic behind a wide, eager smile, genuinely believing this promotion is his chance to learn ethical real estate from a newly successful mentor. He softly expresses his gratitude, whispering that true success comes from matching the right soul to the right sanctuary, completely missing the predatory gleam in Valerie's eye.

Oblivious to his boss's manipulative partnership, Toby immediately gets to work, meticulously organizing the VIP guest list into perfect right angles across his desk pad. He hums a cheerful tune while color-coding the names of the city's most elite buyers, entirely unaware that his earnest dedication is actively curating a tasting menu for a supernatural predator.
[SEQUENCE 3 END]

[SEQUENCE 4 START]
SEQUENCE 4: The Appetite Grows

Valerie ruthlessly weaponizes her new asset, meticulously curating a VIP viewing list comprised entirely of corrupt politicians, predatory landlords, and toxic social media influencers. She hooks her vile clientele by pitching the brutalist mansion as the ultimate, unattainable status symbol, leveraging their immense egos to lure them into the isolated canyon.

The gruesome, architectural executions begin with a notorious slumlord who steps into the mansion's sleek, glass-paneled elevator to inspect the upper floors. The steel cables shriek as the elevator car violently shrinks inward, crushing the screaming landlord into a compact cube before spitting another flawless diamond into the hallway.

Days later, a corrupt city mayor wades into the backyard's stunning infinity pool, arrogantly swirling a glass of expensive scotch. The crystal-clear water suddenly thickens into a gelatinous, sentient mass, dragging the thrashing politician beneath the surface until his struggles cease, leaving behind only a floating gemstone.

Valerie treats the escalating body count as a highly efficient business model, calmly sweeping up the pristine diamonds after each horrific meal. Her nervous complicity fully transforms into euphoric invincibility as she cashes out the gems, replacing her imitation-designer blazer with authentic couture and finally incinerating her pink eviction notice.

Riding a high of arrogant control, Valerie confidently ushers a vile, elderly billionaire into the cavernous foyer, fully expecting the house to consume him and produce her largest payday yet. She flashes a flawless, icy smile, stepping back to let the architectural predator do its bloody work on the unsuspecting tycoon.

Instead of swallowing the billionaire, the floorboards violently buckle and launch the elderly man backward, regurgitating him across the marble floor in a heap of bruised limbs. The heavy oak front doors slam shut with a deafening crack, instantly throwing the deadbolts and trapping Valerie inside the vibrating, angry structure.

The pristine walls begin to pulsate with a sickening, rhythmic heartbeat, bleeding a thick, clear sap that pools across the hardwood. The viscous fluid slowly crawls across the floorboards, deliberately spelling out the name 'TOBY' in jagged, dripping letters. Valerie stares at the supernatural demand, a sudden, sickening realization washing over her as she understands the monster no longer wants terrible people; it demands pure innocence.
[SEQUENCE 4 END]
stage5_treatment.act_2b:
[SEQUENCE 5 START]
SEQUENCE 5: The Moral Compromise

Valerie desperately drags GEMMA (30s), a notoriously cutthroat rival realtor, through the mansion's sprawling corridors. She pitches the property with manic energy, silently praying the house will devour the unethical woman and spare Toby. Gemma merely sneers at the brutalist architecture, loudly complaining about the harsh acoustics and derivative fixtures.

The house violently rejects the offering. Instead of the floorboards opening, the hallway violently constricts, launching a jagged splinter of crown molding directly at Valerie. The wood slashes her cheek, sending a clear, bloody message of refusal. Gemma scoffs at the falling debris and storms out, leaving Valerie bleeding and utterly out of options.

Retreating to the Sterling Real Estate bullpen, Valerie frantically dabs her bleeding face in the restroom mirror. Her moment of respite shatters when RICHARD STERLING (50s), the agency's ruthless founder, corners her against the sinks. He slams down a thick dossier containing the missing persons reports of their most elite, vanished clientele.

Richard physically boxes her in, his expensive cologne suffocating her as he delivers a venomous ultimatum. He demands she host a massive, catered open house gala at the mansion tonight to prove the property is safe and legitimate. If she refuses, he promises to fire her, seize the listing, and hand her over to the police, trapping Valerie between a supernatural predator and total ruin.
[SEQUENCE 5 END]

[SEQUENCE 6 START]
SEQUENCE 6: The Trap is Sprung

Nightfall blankets the canyon as Valerie stands paralyzed at the mansion's threshold, her stomach twisting in knots. Richard shoves past her with a triumphant smirk, ushering in a massive flood of innocent caterers, string quartet musicians, and unsuspecting wealthy socialites. The house hums with a dark, electric anticipation as the pure souls cross its threshold.

Toby bounds up the front steps in an ill-fitting tuxedo, his face beaming with earnest pride at his first major event. He immediately takes charge of the coat check, humming a cheerful melody while carefully hanging expensive jackets. He remains entirely blind to the walls subtly vibrating around him, the plaster practically salivating at his proximity.

Valerie desperately intercepts Richard near the champagne fountain, begging him to cancel the event under the guise of a sudden black mold discovery. Richard laughs directly in her face, raising his crystal flute to toast his own impending, astronomical commission. He dismisses her concerns entirely, cementing the doom of everyone in the room.

The antique grandfather clock in the study chimes a heavy, resonant eight o'clock. Instantly, thick steel security shutters violently crash down over every window and exterior door with a deafening metallic screech. The heavy deadbolts snap into place simultaneously, plunging the vibrant gala into a suffocating, inescapable lockdown.

The architecture unleashes its full, indiscriminate fury upon the trapped crowd. The grand, sweeping staircase suddenly flattens into a steep, frictionless slide, stripping away all footholds. A dozen screaming guests plummet downward, sliding directly into the basement where grinding, unseen industrial gears eagerly await their arrival.

Above the main dining room, the massive crystal chandelier violently detaches from its reinforced ceiling mountings. It plummets directly into the fleeing crowd, its sharp glass pendants skewering a group of terrified caterers. The pristine white rugs instantly soak up a deep, horrific crimson as panic erupts into absolute pandemonium.

Valerie cowers behind the thick marble kitchen island, her trembling hands clutching a heavy velvet bag of her previously collected diamonds. She watches the indiscriminate slaughter unfold through the glass partitions, her breath catching in her throat. The sickening realization washes over her that her insatiable corporate greed directly built this inescapable slaughterhouse.

A deafening crack echoes near the coat check as the hardwood floor violently splits open beneath Toby's feet. He drops into the sudden chasm, his hands desperately catching the jagged floorboards as he dangles over a dark, fleshy abyss. He screams for help, the house finally attempting to claim its demanded, pure prize.

The sight of Toby's impending, gruesome death shatters Valerie's icy, calculating facade completely. She drops the heavy bag of diamonds, the priceless gems scattering uselessly across the blood-stained marble. The weight of her moral bankruptcy crushes her as she lunges out from behind the counter, sprinting toward the chasm to save him.
[SEQUENCE 6 END]
stage5_treatment.act_3:
[SEQUENCE 7 START]
SEQUENCE 7: The Demolition Plan

Valerie hauls Toby by his collar, dragging him into a windowless, steel-reinforced utility closet. She slams the heavy door shut, throwing the deadbolt just as a horrific, wet tearing sound echoes from the hallway outside. Sliding down the cold metal door, Valerie collapses onto the concrete floor, her breath coming in ragged, panicked gasps. Her trembling fingers immediately find the edge of her blazer, obsessively scrubbing at the invisible stains on her cuffs.

The sounds of the massacre bleed through the walls, breaking Valerie's icy composure completely. She chokes back a sob, confessing the entire sickening pact to Toby in a rushed, frantic whisper. She admits she fed innocent people to the architecture for diamonds, realizing with crushing clarity that she has become the exact type of heartless corporate monster that evicted her own family.

Toby kneels beside her, his usual timid demeanor entirely vanished in the claustrophobic dark. He gently reaches out, wrapping his hands around her frantic, scrubbing fingers to force her to stop. He looks her dead in the eye, his stutter completely gone, and tells her that while the system is designed to break people, she holds the power to break the system permanently. The profound truth of his words hits Valerie like a physical blow, igniting a fierce, desperate resolve in her chest.

Channeling his savant-like obsession with structural engineering, Toby pulls up the mansion's original, highly classified blueprints on his cracked smartphone. His fingers fly across the screen, tracing the labyrinthine schematics until he pinpoints a massive, highly pressurized central gas main running beneath the foundation. He maps out a route through the narrow, shifting ventilation shafts, confident he can manually rupture the heavy iron pipes.

Valerie studies the glowing screen, realizing Toby needs absolute, uninterrupted time to sabotage the reinforced valves. She knows the house's primary digestive core lies deep in the basement, and it requires a substantial, living distraction to pull its attention away from the walls. Formulating a suicidal counter-attack, she spots a heavy, red-handled fire axe resting in an emergency glass case on the closet wall.

Valerie shatters the glass with her elbow, wrapping her bleeding hands tightly around the axe's rubberized grip. She shares a final, resolute nod with Toby before boosting him up into the overhead air return grate. As Toby disappears into the dark ductwork, Valerie kicks the utility closet door open, stepping back out into the blood-soaked hallway to hunt down Richard.
[SEQUENCE 7 END]

[SEQUENCE 8 START]
SEQUENCE 8: Closing the Deal

Valerie stalks through the descending corridors, slamming her axe against the walls to draw the architecture's hungry attention downward. She deliberately leaves a trail of bloody footprints, luring a manic, wide-eyed Richard deep into the basement's massive, fleshy furnace room. The sub-level resembles a biomechanical slaughterhouse, its concrete walls pulsing with thick, dark veins and grinding industrial gears.

Richard corners her near the central maw, his expensive suit torn and splattered with gore. Driven entirely mad by greed, he lunges at Valerie with a jagged piece of rebar, screaming that the diamond-producing core belongs exclusively to him. Before his weapon can connect, the house violently turns on them both, the reinforced concrete walls suddenly animating and constricting inward to crush them into the grinding gears.

The floor tilts drastically, sliding both Valerie and Richard toward the snapping, metallic teeth of the central furnace. In a final, desperate act of sacrifice, Valerie unclips the heavy velvet bag of accumulated diamonds from her belt. She hurls the priceless, blood-soaked gems directly into the exposed, churning machinery, the incredibly hard stones instantly jamming the house's crushing mechanism with a deafening screech of tearing metal.

A thick, overwhelming hiss echoes from the ceiling as Toby successfully floods the basement with raw, highly combustible gas. Valerie scrambles backward up the tilted floor, leaving Richard trapped and screaming as the stalled gears begin to slowly grind his legs. She sparks her heavy, engraved corporate lighter, tossing the flame directly into the invisible, toxic fumes.

Valerie dives headfirst into a reinforced steel dumbwaiter just as the spark ignites the gas main. A spectacular, apocalyptic shockwave rips through the foundation, instantly incinerating Richard and obliterating the entity's fleshy core. The massive explosion rockets the dumbwaiter upward through the shaft, ejecting Valerie into the night air as the entire modernist mansion collapses into a fiery, spectacular ruin.

Sirens wail in the distance, cutting through the heavy silence of the canyon. A bruised, soot-covered Valerie sits on the cold concrete curb across the street, her eyes reflecting the towering, smoldering flames of the destroyed property. The oppressive, suffocating weight of the house is entirely gone, leaving only the crackle of burning timber in the cool night air.

Toby limps out of the shadows, his ill-fitting tuxedo scorched and torn, and quietly takes a seat beside her on the curb. He unscrews the cap of a surviving, battered thermos, pouring her a cheap, steaming cup of coffee. Valerie takes the plastic cup, letting the warmth seep into her battered, exhausted hands.

Valerie looks down at her ruined, blood-stained blazer cuffs, the fabric completely destroyed by the night's horrors. For the first time in her life, she makes absolutely no move to scrub them clean. She sits peacefully in the glow of the burning wreckage, entirely broke, completely unbothered, and finally free of her corporate greed.
[SEQUENCE 8 END]

She looks down at her cheap, imitation-designer blazer and begins obsessively scrubbing at an invisible stain on the cuff, her icy professional mask cracking in the privacy of her financial ruin.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie uses the forty-eight-hour window Richard just granted her to quickly pack her briefcase and back out of the conference room.
