scenes.dramaturgical_function: Purpose: Propel Valerie out of the immediate threat of her boss and toward the sequence's catalyst. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to project total control vs. her internal panic over the 48-hour deadline. Value Shift (+/-): From suffocated by Richard's threat (-) to finding a desperate glimmer of a lifeline (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's sputtering, older-model sedan struggles as she forces it up a steep, winding, and completely isolated canyon road. She taps her steering wheel anxiously, checking the cryptic address on her phone mounted to the dashboard as the city skyline disappears entirely behind the rugged terrain.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Purpose: Establish the physical isolation of the monster's lair. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to reach the property quickly vs. the physical limitations of her cheap, failing vehicle. Value Shift (+/-): From determined momentum (+) to creeping geographical isolation (-).

scenes.narrative_action: The sputtering sedan parks in the shadow of a stunning, brutalist modernist mansion. Valerie steps out, her cheap heels crunching on the pristine gravel. She approaches the towering, monolithic grand front doors, pulling a heavy, antique-looking key from the envelope left for her. She slides the key into the lock.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Purpose: The initial threshold crossing into the monster's domain. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to enter and appraise the house vs. the imposing, intimidating physical architecture of the brutalist structure. Value Shift (+/-): From anxious anticipation (-) to awe-struck discovery (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie pushes the heavy doors open and steps into the cavernous foyer. The moment she crosses the threshold, her breath visibly mists in the air. The ambient temperature plummets instantly. She wraps her arms around herself, violently shivering, and looks up at the towering ceiling.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Purpose: Introduce the first physical anomaly of the supernatural entity. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to conduct a standard walkthrough vs. the house's unnatural, freezing environment. Value Shift (+/-): From professional curiosity (+) to sudden physical discomfort (-).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie walks deeper into the house. As she moves down the long corridor, the architectural lines of the walls and ceiling subtly warp and shift around her, creating the dizzying optical illusion of a massive, closing throat. She stumbles, catching herself on the wall. A dark, predatory vibration pulses upward through the pristine hardwood floorboards, resonating directly into the soles of her cheap heels.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Purpose: Escalate the supernatural threat and establish the house's sentience. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to maintain her balance and logic vs. the physically shifting, vibrating reality of the hallway. Value Shift (+/-): From physical discomfort (-) to primal, disorienting terror (-).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie scrambles backward, every primal survival instinct making her pivot toward the front door to run. She stops dead in her tracks. Her hands shaking, she digs into her purse and pulls out a crumpled, bright pink eviction notice. She stares at the paper, then looks back at the sprawling, multi-million-dollar architecture. She aggressively taps numbers into her phone's calculator, staring at the massive commission total. She shoves the eviction notice back into her bag, forcefully rolls her shoulders back, and taps 'CLAIM LISTING' on her real estate app.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Purpose: Cement Valerie's fatal flaw; she actively chooses financial gain over her own safety. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie's primal instinct to flee vs. her desperate need for the commission to avoid eviction. Value Shift (+/-): From terrified flight (-) to ruthless, calculated commitment (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Hours later. A ridiculously loud, neon-colored sports car aggressively crunches to a halt on the gravel driveway. ARTHUR PENDELTON (38) kicks the door open, loudly barking into his phone about a crypto-merger. Valerie stands by the grand front doors, her posture perfectly straight, wearing a flawless, icy professional smile. She extends a glossy brochure to Arthur the moment he hangs up.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Purpose: Introduce the first victim and initiate the 'Debate' beat of the sequence. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to deliver a controlled, sanitized pitch vs. Arthur's loud, chaotic, and dominant entrance. Value Shift (+/-): From prepared isolation (+) to dealing with an unpredictable, obnoxious variable (-).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie ushers Arthur through the echoing corridors, delivering her polished real estate pitch. Arthur booms through the halls, constantly interrupting her to drop obscure luxury brand names. He slaps the walls, openly insulting the minimalist craftsmanship and scoffing at the lack of gold trim. Valerie deflects his insults by pivoting the conversation, pointing out the aesthetic value of the imported marble counters, masking her irritation.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Purpose: Demonstrate Arthur's boorish nature, making him the perfect target for the house. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie attempts to sell the architectural value vs. Arthur actively devaluing the property to inflate his own ego. Value Shift (+/-): From a controlled tour (+) to an aggressively derailed presentation (-).

scenes.narrative_action: Arthur continues his tirade, but his booming voice suddenly cracks. He aggressively shakes his wrist, obsessively checking his heavy luxury watch without ever letting his eyes actually focus on the dial. In direct response to his boorish insults, a heavy oak door at the far end of the hall violently slams shut on its own. The pristine hardwood floorboards beneath their feet groan with a deep, hungry friction.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Purpose: The house reacts to Arthur, merging the supernatural threat with the horrific client. Micro-Conflict (><): Arthur attempts to project overwhelming confidence vs. his own imposter syndrome and the house's sudden, aggressive physical reaction. Value Shift (+/-): From obnoxious dominance (+) to sudden, jarring supernatural threat (-).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stands completely paralyzed in the center of the shifting corridor. She looks at the slammed oak door, then at Arthur, who is momentarily stunned. Her knuckles turn white as she grips her clipboard. She looks down at the calculator app still glowing on her phone screen. She takes a slow, deliberate breath, plasters on a flawless, icy smile, and extends her arm, physically guiding Arthur deeper into the dark, groaning belly of the house.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Purpose: The culmination of the Debate; Valerie makes the active, irreversible choice to feed the client to the house. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie's conscience and fear vs. her overwhelming greed and desperation. Value Shift (+/-): From paralyzed indecision (-) to a dark, damned resolve (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Picking up exactly from the previous moment, Valerie maintains her flawless, icy smile, physically guiding a momentarily stunned Arthur out of the dark, groaning corridor and into the pristine, modernist main living area. Arthur quickly recovers his booming bravado, pacing the expansive room and loudly criticizing the architectural choices to mask his brief moment of unease.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Position the victim for the kill and re-establish the false sense of security. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to keep Arthur compliant and moving vs. Arthur's need to dominate the physical space. Value Shift (+/-): From tense and paralyzed (-) to falsely secure (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Arthur throws his considerable weight against a pristine, load-bearing wall. He gestures wildly, loudly mocking the recessed lighting as cheap and derivative. Valerie stands a few feet away, maintaining her professional mask, her hands tightly gripping her clipboard. Without a single creak of warning, the smooth plaster directly behind Arthur begins to ripple and shift.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Trigger the supernatural threat. Micro-Conflict (><): Arthur wants to show off his superior taste vs. the house's awakening, predatory hunger. Value Shift (+/-): From arrogant dominance (+) to oblivious peril (-).

scenes.narrative_action: The wall liquefies into a jagged, fleshy maw, its architectural lines snapping into rows of calcified teeth. The house violently inhales. Arthur screams as he is dragged backward into the drywall. In a matter of seconds, the wall swallows him whole, digesting his entire body and snapping back into a smooth, pristine surface as if nothing happened.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Deliver the violent, shocking explosion of action and establish the monster's lethality. Micro-Conflict (><): Arthur fights for his life vs. the house's overwhelming supernatural strength. Value Shift (+/-): From alive (+) to digested (-).

scenes.narrative_action: Blind, primal terror seizes Valerie. She drops her clipboard and her professional veneer entirely, gasping for air as she scrambles frantically down the hallway. She propels herself into the foyer, lunging toward the heavy oak front doors, yanking at the handles in a desperate bid to escape the sentient trap.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Execute the protagonist's natural survival reaction to the trauma. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to flee the house immediately vs. the physical distance to the exit. Value Shift (+/-): From composed professional (-) to sheer, unadulterated terror (-).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's desperate flight halts abruptly when a sickening, wet cough echoes from the center of the foyer, shattering the dead silence. She freezes, turning slowly. A massive, perfectly cut, blood-free diamond clatters onto the hardwood floor. It spins like a top across the wood, coming to a dead stop exactly at the tip of her cheap heel.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Introduce the monster's transactional nature and the bait for the protagonist's fatal flaw. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie's urge to run vs. the hypnotic, impossible allure of the gemstone. Value Shift (+/-): From panicked flight (-) to stunned curiosity (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stares down at the impossible gemstone. Her trembling fingers instinctively rise, obsessively scrubbing at an invisible stain on her blazer cuff. The sheer terror in her chest rapidly cools. Her eyes narrow as her mind tallies the astronomical value of the stone. She reaches down, scoops the diamond into her pocket, and stands up straight, sealing a dark, silent pact with the entity.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The Break into Two - the protagonist commits the ultimate sin of the genre for personal gain. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie's basic human morality vs. her desperate, deeply rooted greed. Value Shift (+/-): From sheer terror (-) to cold, calculating greed (+).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STERLING REAL ESTATE - BULLPEN - THE NEXT MORNING
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie strides into the chaotic Sterling Real Estate bullpen radiating a dangerous, untouchable confidence. Her pocket is visibly heavy with newly acquired cash. She ignores the usual office chatter, bypassing her own cubicle entirely, and marches directly across the floor, zeroing in on Toby's desk.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establish Valerie's new empowered and corrupted status quo. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to assert her new dominance vs. the chaotic, oppressive office environment that usually grinds her down. Value Shift (+/-): From financially drowning (-) to flush with cash and untouchable power (+).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STERLING REAL ESTATE - TOBY'S DESK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie slams a thick stack of high-end cardstock down in front of Toby, demanding he act as the exclusive concierge for her new invite-only property. Toby, masking his crushing financial panic, flashes a wide, eager smile. He softly expresses his gratitude, whispering that true success comes from matching the right soul to the right sanctuary, completely missing the predatory gleam in Valerie's eye.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The B Story beat - introducing the moral counterweight and initiating the manipulative partnership. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to use Toby's skills for her dark agenda vs. Toby's earnest desire to learn ethical real estate. Value Shift (+/-): From isolated guilt (-) to a manipulative, active partnership (+).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STERLING REAL ESTATE - TOBY'S DESK - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Oblivious to the dark reality of his new role, Toby immediately gets to work. He meticulously organizes the high-end VIP guest list into perfect right angles across his desk pad. Humming a cheerful tune, he color-codes the names of the city's most elite buyers, entirely unaware that his earnest dedication is actively curating a tasting menu for a supernatural predator.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Lock in the fatal dramatic irony of the B Story. Micro-Conflict (><): Toby's psychological need for order and ethical success vs. the inherently doomed, deadly nature of his task. Value Shift (+/-): From anxious about his career (-) to cheerfully, yet tragically, purposeful (+).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STERLING REAL ESTATE - TOBY'S DESK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Toby meticulously aligns the high-end VIP guest list into perfect right angles on his desk pad. Valerie approaches, her eyes locking onto the curated names. She physically pulls the list from Toby's hands, her finger bypassing the honest buyers and tracing down to the name of a notorious slumlord. Toby stutters, nervously organizing his pens while trying to suggest a nice, deserving family instead. Valerie cuts him off, deploying sharp real estate buzzwords about 'aggressive portfolio synergy' to sanitize her grim choice, officially selecting her first targeted meal.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes Valerie's active choice to weaponize the house by deliberately targeting the most corrupt clients on Toby's innocent list.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie leads the loud, notorious SLUMLORD into the brutalist mansion. He immediately complains about the cold aesthetic, taking up physical space and interrupting her pitch. Valerie maintains a flawless, icy professional veneer, pitching the 'unyielding exclusivity' of the property. She guides him toward the sleek, glass-paneled elevator to inspect the upper floors, but deliberately stops short, gesturing for him to step inside alone while she remains safely in the foyer.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Sets up the first deliberate execution, testing whether Valerie can successfully control and feed the sentient house on command.

scenes.narrative_action: The Slumlord hits the button. The steel cables shriek violently. The glass walls of the elevator car suddenly shrink inward with terrifying speed. The Slumlord screams as he is crushed into a compact, bloody cube against the glass. A moment later, a flawless, pristine diamond clatters onto the hallway floor. Valerie steps forward, her icy mask breaking into a euphoric smile as she calmly sweeps the diamond into her pocket.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Delivers the promise of the premise—the horrific architectural kill and the pristine financial reward.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie slides the blood-free diamond across a velvet appraisal pad. The Appraiser inspects it through a loupe, stunned by its impossible clarity. He hands over thick stacks of hundred-dollar bills. Valerie's hands tremble slightly as she packs the cash into her designer bag, masking her underlying desperation with a sharp, transactional nod before exiting the shop.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Translates the supernatural reward into tangible, life-changing wealth, fueling Valerie's addiction to the deadly process.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stands in her cramped, dingy kitchen, now wearing a brand-new, authentic couture blazer. She retrieves her bright pink eviction notice from the counter. With a steady hand, she holds the notice over the open flame of her gas stove, watching it incinerate into ash. She exhales deeply, the heavy, lifelong weight of her childhood poverty temporarily lifting from her shoulders.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Resolves Valerie's immediate external threat (eviction) and cements her false belief that wealth provides absolute safety.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie hosts a CORRUPT MAYOR. He arrogantly wades into the stunning infinity pool, swirling a glass of expensive scotch and talking down to her. Valerie stands safely on the patio edge in her pristine couture, checking her watch and treating the interaction as a highly efficient, transactional business model while waiting for the house to strike.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalates the montage of kills, demonstrating Valerie's complete desensitization to murder as she treats it like a routine business transaction.

scenes.narrative_action: The crystal-clear pool water suddenly thickens into a gelatinous, sentient mass. The Mayor thrashes wildly, spilling his scotch, but the mass drags him violently beneath the surface. The thrashing stops. A massive gemstone floats to the top of the viscous water. Valerie calmly picks up a long aluminum pool skimmer and fishes the diamond out of the water.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Demonstrates the house's growing, creative power and Valerie's normalized complicity in the gruesome deaths.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STERLING REAL ESTATE - VALERIE'S DESK - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie, radiating arrogant control, reviews the VIP list for her next target. Gemma Frost approaches, dripping with fake sweetness, and delivers a passive-aggressive jab about Valerie's sudden wealth while checking her reflection in her phone screen. Valerie effortlessly shuts her down by pivoting the conversation to the aesthetic superiority of her new couture. Gemma aggressively picks at her cuticle until it bleeds and walks away. Toby cheerfully hands Valerie the file for a vile, elderly billionaire.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes Valerie's peak hubris before the fall and sets up the midpoint target, contrasting her corruption with Toby's innocence.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie confidently ushers the vile, ELDERLY BILLIONAIRE into the cavernous foyer. She flashes a flawless, icy smile, anticipating her largest payday yet. She takes a deliberate, practiced step back toward the front door, leaving the billionaire standing alone in the center of the room, waiting for the architecture to consume him.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The false victory setup; Valerie believes she is entirely in control of the monster and the transactional rules.

scenes.narrative_action: The floorboards violently buckle. Instead of swallowing the billionaire, the house launches him backward. He is regurgitated across the marble floor in a heap of bruised limbs, groaning in pain but entirely alive. Valerie's icy veneer shatters. She obsessively scrubs at her expensive cuffs, staring in total shock at the rejected meal.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The midpoint reversal; the house violently changes the rules of the transaction, rejecting the terrible person.

scenes.narrative_action: The heavy oak front doors slam shut with a deafening crack. The deadbolts throw themselves, locking Valerie and the groaning billionaire inside. The pristine walls begin to pulsate with a sickening, rhythmic heartbeat, bleeding a thick, clear sap. The viscous fluid crawls across the floorboards, deliberately spelling out the name 'TOBY' in jagged, dripping letters. Valerie stares at the supernatural demand, horrified.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The midpoint climax; the monster traps Valerie and demands pure innocence, completely stripping her of control and escalating the stakes.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stares in horror at the viscous sap spelling 'TOBY' on the floorboards. The groaning billionaire from the previous showing is abruptly yanked under the floorboards with a wet crunch, leaving behind a single, pristine diamond. Valerie snatches the diamond, frantically scrubbing the sap letters away with her bare hands. She screams at the pulsating walls that Toby is off-limits, promising to bring the house someone much worse. The heavy oak doors unlatch with a reluctant click.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establishes the house's specific demand and Valerie's moral line in the sand. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie wants to escape with her life vs. the house demanding the sacrifice of her innocent assistant. Value Shift: (+/-) Horrified to Determined.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STERLING REAL ESTATE - BULLPEN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie marches into the bustling office bullpen, her eyes locking onto Gemma, who is aggressively picking her cuticles while scrolling her phone. Valerie slaps a glossy mansion brochure onto Gemma's desk, masking her terror with a flawless, icy professional veneer. She pitches a 'lucrative co-listing preview,' using rapid-fire real estate buzzwords to bait Gemma's ego. Gemma checks her reflection in her phone screen, suspicious, but grabs her designer coat.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Sets up the substitute sacrifice to appease the house. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie needs to lure Gemma to the property vs. Gemma's natural distrust of Valerie's motives. Value Shift: (+/-) Desperate to Hopeful.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's car screeches into the driveway. Gemma steps out, immediately sneering at the brutalist exterior and complaining about the harsh landscaping. Valerie ignores the insults, her eyes fixed on the front door, silently begging the house to cooperate and consume her rival. She unlocks the door, physically gesturing for Gemma to enter first.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Delivers the sacrifice to the altar. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie wants to get Gemma inside quickly vs. Gemma dragging her feet to critique the property. Value Shift: (+/-) Hopeful to Anxious.

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie leads Gemma into the cavernous living space, her pitch taking on a manic, breathless energy. She physically maneuvers Gemma toward the center of the room, right over the floorboards that previously opened. Gemma loudly complains about the harsh acoustics and derivative fixtures, completely oblivious to the danger. Valerie waits, sweating, staring at the floor, but the wood remains perfectly solid.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Demonstrates the house's refusal to eat toxic people. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie tries to position Gemma over the trap vs. the house refusing to activate for an unworthy soul. Value Shift: (+/-) Anxious to Frustrated.

scenes.narrative_action: Desperate, Valerie drags Gemma deeper into the sprawling corridors, pointing out arbitrary architectural details. Gemma rolls her eyes, slowly reapplying lip gloss while mocking Valerie's desperation. Suddenly, the house emits a low, structural groan. The walls of the hallway begin to subtly constrict, warping the perspective. Valerie backs away, expecting Gemma to be swallowed, but the floorboards beneath Gemma remain tightly shut.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Escalates the supernatural threat. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie wants the house to take Gemma vs. the house preparing to strike back at Valerie instead. Value Shift: (+/-) Frustrated to Terrified.

scenes.narrative_action: The hallway violently snaps inward. Instead of opening a pit, the ceiling buckles, launching a jagged, spear-like splinter of crown molding directly at Valerie. She dives, but the wood slashes her cheek, drawing a stream of blood. Gemma scoffs at the falling debris, loudly criticizing the 'shoddy craftsmanship,' and turns on her heel, storming out of the house. Valerie is left bleeding on the floor.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The physical rejection of the offering and the monster turning on its master. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie tries to survive the house's retaliation vs. Gemma's total apathy. Value Shift: (+/-) Terrified to Defeated.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STERLING REAL ESTATE - BULLPEN - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie sneaks back into the office, pressing a bloody tissue to her cheek. Toby spots her and rushes over, his stutter flaring up in panic as he offers to get a first-aid kit. Knowing the house wants him, Valerie violently shoves him away, snapping at him to mind his own business and stop being so pathetic. Toby recoils, hurt, compulsively organizing the pens on a nearby desk into perfect right angles, while Valerie flees toward the restrooms.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Isolates Valerie from her only ally to protect him. Micro-Conflict: (><) Toby wants to care for Valerie vs. Valerie needing to cruelly push him away to save his life. Value Shift: (+/-) Defeated to Isolated.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STERLING REAL ESTATE - WOMEN'S RESTROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie bursts into the empty restroom, leaning over the sink. She drops her icy professional veneer, hyperventilating as she frantically dabs the blood from her slashed cheek with a paper towel. Her panic peaks, and she begins obsessively, violently scrubbing at invisible stains on her expensive white cuffs, staring wide-eyed at her own terrified reflection in the mirror.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Shows Valerie hitting rock bottom emotionally before the final blow. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie tries to regain her physical composure vs. her overwhelming trauma and fear. Value Shift: (+/-) Isolated to Broken.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STERLING REAL ESTATE - WOMEN'S RESTROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The restroom door swings open. Richard Sterling steps inside, casually locking the deadbolt behind him. Valerie freezes. Richard slowly approaches, his expensive cologne suffocating the small space, and slams a thick dossier onto the wet counter. The pages spill out: missing persons reports for the elite clients Valerie has been 'selling' to.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Introduces the human antagonist's trap. Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie tries to maintain her lie vs. Richard presenting undeniable proof of her crimes. Value Shift: (+/-) Broken to Cornered.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STERLING REAL ESTATE - WOMEN'S RESTROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Richard physically boxes Valerie in against the sinks, invading her personal space. He whispers through clenched teeth, stripping away his sophisticated facade. He issues his ultimatum: Valerie will host a massive, catered open house gala at the mansion tonight to prove it's safe. If she refuses, he will fire her, seize the listing, and hand the dossier to the police. Trapped, Valerie slowly nods.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Locks Valerie into the Act II climax (the gala). Micro-Conflict: (><) Valerie wants to avoid returning to the house vs. Richard forcing her back in with a massive crowd. Value Shift: (+/-) Cornered to Doomed.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. STERLING REAL ESTATE - WOMEN'S RESTROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Picking up the exact second Richard forces her compliance, he steps back, adjusts his cuffs with a triumphant smirk, and exits the restroom. Left alone, Valerie stares at her reflection in the mirror, her breathing shallow and panicked. Her icy mask completely crumbles. She obsessively scrubs at an invisible stain on her expensive cuffs, terrified by the realization that she must now feed a house full of innocent people to a monster to save her own career.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establish Valerie's total loss of control following the ultimatum. Micro-Conflict: Valerie vs. her own crippling guilt and panic. Value Shift: From trapped but composed (+) to completely unraveling (-).

scenes.narrative_action: Nightfall blankets the canyon. Valerie stands paralyzed at the threshold of the modernist mansion, her stomach twisting in knots as catering vans idle in the driveway. Richard arrives and aggressively shoves past her, his face locked in a predatory, victorious smile. He ushers in a massive flood of innocent caterers, string quartet musicians, and unsuspecting wealthy socialites. The house hums with a dark, electric anticipation, the walls practically vibrating as pure souls cross the threshold.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Set the stage for the massacre and introduce the innocent victims. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants to delay the inevitable vs. Richard forcing the event forward. Value Shift: From hesitant anticipation (+) to paralyzed dread (-).

scenes.narrative_action: Toby bounds up the front steps wearing an ill-fitting tuxedo, his face beaming with earnest pride at working his first major luxury event. He immediately takes charge of the coat check in the foyer, humming a cheerful melody while carefully hanging expensive jackets. He remains entirely blind to the terrifying reality around him: the plaster walls are subtly vibrating and practically salivating at his close proximity.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Place the moral compass directly in the monster's crosshairs. Micro-Conflict: Toby wants to do a good job vs. the house wanting to consume him. Value Shift: From earnest excitement (+) to unknowingly endangered (-).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - MAIN HALL - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: The gala is packed and buzzing. Valerie desperately intercepts Richard near the towering champagne fountain. Panic bleeding through her professional tone, she begs him to cancel the event, lying that she just discovered toxic black mold in the vents. Richard laughs directly in her face. He raises his crystal flute to toast his own impending, astronomical commission, entirely dismissing her concerns and cementing the doom of everyone in the room.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Valerie's final, failed attempt to stop the monster. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants to cancel the gala vs. Richard demanding his commission. Value Shift: From desperate hope (+) to absolute defeat (-).

scenes.narrative_action: The antique grandfather clock in the study chimes a heavy, resonant eight o clock. The moment the final chime echoes, thick steel security shutters violently crash down over every single window and exterior door with a deafening metallic screech. Heavy deadbolts snap into place simultaneously across the entire property, plunging the vibrant gala into a suffocating, inescapable lockdown. The crowd falls dead silent.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Spring the physical trap. Micro-Conflict: The guests want to enjoy the party vs. the house locking them inside. Value Shift: From oblivious celebration (+) to sudden, suffocating panic (-).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The architecture unleashes its full, indiscriminate fury upon the trapped crowd. The grand, sweeping staircase suddenly flattens into a steep, frictionless slide, stripping away all footholds in an instant. A dozen screaming guests lose their balance and plummet downward, sliding at terrifying speeds directly into the basement where grinding, unseen industrial gears roar to life, eagerly awaiting their arrival.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Initiate the feeding frenzy. Micro-Conflict: Guests want to escape vs. the house altering its architecture to kill them. Value Shift: From desperate attempt to flee (+) to inescapable doom (-).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - MAIN DINING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Above the main dining room, the massive crystal chandelier violently detaches from its reinforced ceiling mountings. It plummets directly into the fleeing crowd below, its sharp glass pendants skewering a group of terrified caterers. The pristine white rugs instantly soak up a deep, horrific crimson as the guests realization of being trapped turns into absolute, bloody pandemonium.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Escalate the slaughter and visually represent the cost of greed. Micro-Conflict: Caterers try to survive vs. the house weaponizing its decor. Value Shift: From terrified survival (+) to bloody devastation (-).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie cowers behind the thick marble kitchen island, her trembling hands tightly clutching a heavy velvet bag filled with her previously collected, blood-free diamonds. She watches the indiscriminate slaughter unfold through the glass partitions, her breath catching in her throat. The sickening realization washes over her that her insatiable corporate greed directly built this inescapable slaughterhouse.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Force Valerie to confront her moral bankruptcy. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants to hide with her wealth vs. the undeniable visual proof of her sins. Value Shift: From clinging to her payout (+) to being crushed by guilt (-).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - COAT CHECK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: A deafening crack echoes near the coat check. The hardwood floor violently splits open beneath Tobys feet. He drops into the sudden chasm, his hands desperately catching the jagged floorboards at the very last second. He dangles helplessly over a dark, fleshy abyss, screaming for help as the house finally attempts to claim its demanded, pure prize.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Target the ultimate innocent to force Valerie's choice. Micro-Conflict: Toby wants to hold on vs. the house pulling him into the abyss. Value Shift: From safe on the sidelines (+) to dangling over death (-).

scenes.narrative_action: The sight of Tobys impending, gruesome death shatters Valeries calculating facade completely. She looks at the heavy bag of diamonds in her hands, then drops it. The priceless gems scatter uselessly across the blood-stained marble floor. The weight of her moral bankruptcy crushes her as she lunges out from behind the safety of the counter, sprinting directly into the chaotic slaughter to save him.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Execute Valerie's core moral awakening. Micro-Conflict: Valerie wants to save her own life and wealth vs. her need to save Toby. Value Shift: From cowardly and greedy (-) to selfless and active (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie abandons the scattered diamonds on the blood-stained marble, sprinting directly into the chaotic slaughter. She tackles Toby just as a section of the modernist wall attempts to crush and consume him. Grabbing him tightly by the collar of his cheap suit, she hauls him away from the snapping architecture and drags him toward a dark side corridor.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: To seamlessly bridge the previous climax into the immediate retreat and establish Valerie's active choice to save a life over wealth. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to physically extract Toby vs. The house's active, shifting architecture trying to consume him. Value Shift (+/-): From paralyzed, guilty bystander (-) to active, physical rescuer (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drags a disoriented, stumbling Toby down the blood-slicked hallway. The walls shift and grind behind them. A horrific, wet tearing sound echoes from the foyer as another guest is consumed off-screen. Valerie spots a heavy, steel-reinforced door marked for utility access and violently shoves Toby toward it.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: To escalate the immediate physical threat and force the characters into a confined, inescapable space for the emotional turning point. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to find immediate physical cover vs. The predatory, closing hallway. Value Shift (+/-): From exposed to immediate danger (-) to finding temporary, fragile shelter (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie shoves Toby inside the windowless room, slams the heavy metal door shut, and throws the deadbolt. The wet tearing sounds from the hallway are muffled but still present. Valerie's icy composure shatters. She slides down the cold metal door, hits the concrete floor, and begins hyperventilating. Her trembling fingers immediately find the edge of her blazer, obsessively scrubbing at the invisible stains on her cuffs.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: To establish the physical and psychological setting for the Dark Night of the Soul. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to regain her flawless professional composure vs. Her overwhelming guilt and panic physicalizing in her scrubbing tic. Value Shift (+/-): From adrenaline-fueled survival (+) to paralyzing, traumatic panic (-).

scenes.narrative_action: As Toby tries to ask what is happening, Valerie chokes back a sob and frantically whispers the entire sickening pact. She confesses that she traded the clients to the architecture for the diamonds. Staring at her scrubbing hands, she tearfully admits she has become the exact type of heartless corporate monster that evicted her family when she was a child.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The core confession of the protagonist's sin, stripping away her 'Lie' completely. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to punish herself with the brutal truth vs. Toby's initial shock and confusion. Value Shift (+/-): From secretive and burdened (-) to utterly exposed and morally broken (- -).

scenes.narrative_action: Toby kneels on the concrete beside her. His timid, subservient demeanor vanishes. He reaches out and physically wraps his hands around Valerie's frantic, scrubbing fingers, forcing her to stop. Looking her dead in the eye without a single stutter, he states that the system is designed to break people, but she holds the power to break the system permanently.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The thematic turnaround and B-story integration, where the Catalyst provides the moral solution. Micro-Conflict (><): Toby wants to snap Valerie out of her self-loathing spiral vs. Valerie's deep-seated belief that she is irredeemable. Value Shift (+/-): From broken despair (-) to a sparked, desperate resolve (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Channeling his savant-like obsession, Toby pulls out his cracked smartphone. His fingers fly across the screen as he pulls up the mansion's original, highly classified blueprints. He traces the labyrinthine schematics until he pinpoints a massive, highly pressurized central gas main running directly beneath the foundation.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Formulating the mechanical blueprint to destroy the monster. Micro-Conflict (><): Toby wants to find a fatal architectural flaw vs. The complex, encrypted nature of the classified schematics. Value Shift (+/-): From trapped victims (-) to proactive saboteurs armed with a plan (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie studies the glowing screen over Toby's shoulder. She points to the basement, identifying it as the house's primary digestive core. Realizing Toby needs absolute, uninterrupted time to navigate the shifting ventilation shafts and manually rupture the heavy iron pipes, she states she must create a suicidal distraction to pull the entity's attention away from the walls.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Dividing the labor for the Break into Three, setting up the dual-pronged climax. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie's need to protect Toby's sabotage mission vs. The house's omnipresent awareness of their location. Value Shift (+/-): From a unified front (+) to a dangerous, divided suicide mission (-).

scenes.narrative_action: Formulating her counter-attack, Valerie scans the room and spots an emergency glass case on the wall holding a heavy, red-handled fire axe. She steps up to it, drives her elbow violently through the glass, and pulls the axe free. Her bleeding hands wrap tightly around the rubberized grip as she tests its heavy weight.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The physical arming of the protagonist, transitioning her from a real estate agent to a monster hunter. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to arm herself vs. The physical barrier of the reinforced glass case. Value Shift (+/-): From physically defenseless (-) to armed and dangerous (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie moves to the overhead air return grate. She intertwines her bleeding fingers, creating a step, and physically boosts Toby upward. Toby pries the metal grate loose and pulls himself up into the dark ductwork. They share a final, resolute nod before Toby disappears into the shadows of the ventilation shaft.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Launching the B-story character into his solo sabotage mission. Micro-Conflict (><): Toby's lack of physical strength vs. The height and heavy screws of the ventilation grate. Value Shift (+/-): From grounded and trapped (-) to successfully infiltrated into the house's nervous system (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stands alone in the closet. She tightens her grip on the bloody axe handle, kicks the heavy utility door open with her heel, and steps back out into the blood-soaked hallway. She steps over a pile of shattered drywall and gore, her eyes locked forward, actively hunting down her predatory boss, Richard.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The final transition into the Act III climax, propelling the protagonist into direct confrontation with the Main Opponent. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to hunt Richard and draw the house's attention vs. The terrifying, actively hostile environment of the hallway. Value Shift (+/-): From hiding in sanctuary (-) to stepping onto the battlefield as an apex predator (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie, gripping the bloody axe, stalks down the shattered, blood-soaked hallway. She deliberately drags the heavy axe blade against the pristine drywall, gouging the architecture to draw the house's attention. She leaves a deliberate trail of bloody footprints, turning toward the basement stairwell to bait Richard into following her.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Establish Valerie's offensive strategy and bait the final trap. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to make enough noise to lure her predators vs. the house's silent, oppressive threat. Value Shift (+/-): From defensive hunting (-) to actively setting the trap (+).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - BASEMENT STAIRWELL - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie descends into the darkening sub-level. The sleek modernist architecture gives way to fleshy, pulsing concrete veins. Above her, the frantic, manic footsteps of Richard echo down the stairwell—he took the bait. Valerie pushes through a heavy steel door into the furnace room.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Transition the protagonist into the belly of the beast. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to reach the core before Richard catches her vs. the terrifying, shifting biomechanical environment. Value Shift (+/-): From confident descent (+) to entering the claustrophobic danger zone (-).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FURNACE ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie enters the massive, biomechanical slaughterhouse. Grinding industrial gears surround a central, snapping maw. Richard bursts through the steel door behind her. His expensive suit is torn and splattered with gore. Driven mad by greed, he spots Valerie and tightens his grip on a jagged piece of rebar.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Initiate the final confrontation between protagonist and antagonist. Micro-Conflict (><): Richard wants to corner Valerie vs. Valerie positioning herself near the deadly gears. Value Shift (+/-): From prepared (+) to physically threatened by a manic killer (-).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FURNACE ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Richard screams that the diamond-producing core belongs exclusively to him. He lunges at Valerie, swinging the rebar. Valerie dodges, parrying with her axe, but Richard's manic strength forces her backward. Before he can deliver a crushing blow, the fleshy concrete walls violently animate, groaning and constricting inward to crush them both.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Escalate the climax by having the monster turn on both humans. Micro-Conflict (><): Richard wants to murder Valerie vs. the house wanting to consume them both. Value Shift (+/-): From human vs. human (-) to human vs. monster (--).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FURNACE ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The floor tilts drastically. Valerie and Richard lose their footing, sliding violently toward the snapping metallic teeth of the central furnace. Richard drops his rebar, desperately clawing at the fleshy floor veins. Valerie slides closer to the maw, her boots scraping uselessly against the slick, tilted concrete.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Push the characters to the absolute brink of consumption. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie and Richard want to survive vs. the house's gravitational pull into the grinding gears. Value Shift (+/-): From fighting each other (-) to fighting gravity and the maw (--).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FURNACE ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Dangling over the edge of the churning machinery, Valerie unclips the heavy velvet bag of accumulated diamonds from her belt. She stares at the priceless, blood-soaked gems—her ticket to financial security—then hurls the entire bag directly into the exposed gears. The incredibly hard stones instantly jam the crushing mechanism with a deafening screech of tearing metal.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The ultimate sacrifice; the protagonist gives up her wealth to stop the monster. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to stop the gears vs. her own ingrained greed and the machine's momentum. Value Shift (+/-): From helpless (-) to seizing control through sacrifice (+).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FURNACE ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: A thick, overwhelming hiss echoes from the ceiling as Toby successfully floods the basement with raw, highly combustible gas. Valerie scrambles backward up the tilted floor. Richard remains trapped below, screaming in agony as the stalled gears slowly begin to grind his legs.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: Introduce the ticking clock and explosive element. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to escape the gas and gears vs. the steep, slippery floor and toxic fumes. Value Shift (+/-): From stalled danger (+) to imminent explosive death (-).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FURNACE ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie reaches a reinforced steel dumbwaiter set into the upper wall. She pulls out her heavy, engraved corporate lighter. She looks back at Richard being consumed by the grinding teeth, sparks the flame, and tosses the lighter directly into the invisible, toxic fumes. She dives headfirst into the dumbwaiter.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The ignition point; protagonist executes the monster and the antagonist. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to ignite the gas and escape vs. the rapidly closing window of survival. Value Shift (+/-): From trapped (-) to explosive escape (+).

scenes.scene_heading: INT/EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DUMBWAITER SHAFT / NIGHT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: A spectacular, apocalyptic shockwave rips through the foundation. The explosion instantly incinerates Richard and obliterates the fleshy core. The massive blast rockets the dumbwaiter upward through the shaft, ejecting Valerie into the night air as the entire modernist mansion collapses into a fiery, spectacular ruin below her.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The explosive climax and physical destruction of the house. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie wants to survive the blast vs. the kinetic, destructive force of the explosion. Value Shift (+/-): From claustrophobic peril (-) to chaotic, airborne liberation (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Sirens wail in the distance. A bruised, soot-covered Valerie sits on the cold concrete curb across the street, watching the smoldering flames of the destroyed property. Toby limps out of the shadows in his scorched tuxedo and sits beside her. He pours her a cheap cup of coffee from a battered thermos. Valerie takes it, letting the warmth seep into her hands.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The aftermath; establishing survival and reconnecting the protagonist with her moral compass. Micro-Conflict (><): Toby wants to comfort Valerie vs. the overwhelming shock of the trauma they just endured. Value Shift (+/-): From explosive chaos (+) to shared, exhausted comfort (+).

scenes.narrative_action: Valerie looks down at her ruined, blood-stained blazer cuffs—the ultimate symbol of her corporate armor, now destroyed. For the first time in her life, she makes absolutely no move to scrub them clean. She takes a sip of the cheap coffee, sitting peacefully in the glow of the burning wreckage, entirely broke, completely unbothered, and finally free.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Structural Purpose: The final thematic image; the protagonist's moral completion and release of her fatal flaw. Micro-Conflict (><): Valerie's ingrained psychological habit to clean her cuffs vs. her newfound internal peace. Value Shift (+/-): From caring about status (-) to total acceptance of her ruined state (+).
